name: 涼麵 (liáng miàn)
description: Cold sesame or soy-sauce noodles served at room temperature — cooling and satisfying on sweltering days

name: 愛玉冰 (ài yù bīng)
description: Chilled fig-jelly dessert in lemon syrup — a quintessential summer refresher

name: 涼拌小黃瓜 (liáng bàn xiǎo huáng guā)
description: Marinated cucumber with garlic, sesame and chilli — crisp and cooling as a side dish

name: 竹筍湯 (zhú sǔn tāng)
description: Light pork and bamboo shoot broth — refreshing and mildly sweet; best in summer when shoots are fresh

name: 豆花 (dòu huā)
description: Silken tofu pudding with shaved ice and sweet syrup — a classic Taiwanese cooling dessert

name: 仙草凍 (xiān cǎo dòng)
description: Grass jelly cubes in cold sugar water — bitter-sweet and deeply cooling

name: 剉冰 (cuò bīng)
description: Shaved ice piled with red bean, taro balls, or fresh mango — the definitive Taiwanese summer treat

name: 綠豆湯 (lǜ dòu tāng)
description: Chilled mung bean sweet soup — traditional cooling food believed to clear heat

name: 冷豆腐 (lěng dòu fǔ)
description: Cold silken tofu topped with soy sauce, sesame oil, and bonito flakes — minimal effort, maximum cool

name: 鮮蝦粥 (xiān xiā zhōu)
description: Light fresh shrimp congee seasoned with ginger and scallion — easy on the stomach on hot mornings

name: 絲瓜蛤蜊 (sī guā gé lì)
description: Luffa gourd simmered with clams in clear broth — sweet, light, and hydrating

name: 麻醬麵 (má jiàng miàn)
description: Noodles tossed in rich sesame paste with cucumber strips — served at room temperature

name: 冬瓜排骨湯 (dōng guā pái gǔ tāng)
description: Winter melon and pork rib soup — subtly sweet, clearing and hydrating in humid heat

name: 滷味 (lǔ wèi)
description: Chilled braised snacks — tofu, duck wings, seaweed — eaten cold from a night-market stall

name: 日式海苔飯糰 (rì shì hǎi tái fàn tuán)
description: Japanese-style seaweed rice ball with tuna or umeboshi — portable and light for sticky-weather days

name: 薑黃飯配炒青菜 (jiāng huáng fàn pèi chǎo qīng cài)
description: Turmeric rice served alongside stir-fried greens — vibrant, aromatic, and easy to digest

name: 清蒸海鮮 (qīng zhēng hǎi xiān)
description: Whole fish or prawns steamed with ginger and scallion — clean flavour, no heavy sauce

name: 米苔目湯 (mǐ tái mù tāng)
description: Slippery rice-flour noodles in a mild pork bone broth — cooling in texture and light on the body

name: 蛤蜊湯 (gé lì tāng)
description: Steamed clam broth with ginger and basil — mineral-rich, clear, and refreshing

name: 拌麵 (bàn miàn)
description: Scallion oil or black sesame noodles served at room temperature — simple, fragrant, satisfying

name: 滷肉飯 (lǔ ròu fàn)
description: Slow-braised minced pork belly over white rice — the soul of Taiwanese comfort food

name: 蚵仔煎 (ô-á-tsian)
description: Crispy oyster omelette with sweet potato starch and egg — a night-market staple for pleasant evenings

name: 鹹酥雞 (yán sū jī)
description: Crispy fried popcorn chicken with basil — the iconic Taiwanese fried snack best enjoyed outdoors

name: 便當 (biàn dāng)
description: Bento box with rice, braised pork, pickled vegetables, and seasonal sides — hearty midday fuel

name: 水餃 (shuǐ jiǎo)
description: Boiled pork and cabbage dumplings with black vinegar — satisfying and versatile across mild to cool days

name: 炒米粉 (chǎo mǐ fěn)
description: Stir-fried rice vermicelli with pork, cabbage, and shiitake — a classic Taiwanese quick lunch

name: 蛋炒飯 (dàn chǎo fàn)
description: Egg fried rice with scallions — quick, fragrant, and endlessly satisfying

name: 台式炒麵 (tái shì chǎo miàn)
description: Taiwanese wok-fried wheat noodles with pork, bean sprouts, and soy sauce — earthy and robust

name: 三杯雞 (sān bēi jī)
description: Chicken braised in equal parts sesame oil, soy sauce, and rice wine with fresh basil — fragrant and intense

name: 刈包 (guà bāo)
description: Soft steamed bun stuffed with braised pork belly, pickled mustard, and peanut powder — the Taiwanese hamburger

name: 豬血糕 (zhū xiě gāo)
description: Steamed pork blood cake skewered and coated in peanut flour and coriander — a uniquely Taiwanese snack

name: 大腸包小腸 (dà cháng bāo xiǎo cháng)
description: Grilled pork sausage wrapped in sticky glutinous rice sausage — savoury, sweet, and filling

name: 肉圓 (bá-wán)
description: Steamed or fried translucent dumpling filled with pork and bamboo — gelatinous and saucy

name: 碗粿 (bán guě)
description: Steamed rice-flour pudding topped with minced pork and dried shrimp — mild, comforting, old-school

name: 客家小炒 (kè jiā xiǎo chǎo)
description: Hakka dry-wok stir-fry with dried squid, pork belly, tofu, and garlic — bold and savoury

name: 排骨飯 (pái gǔ fàn)
description: Crispy fried pork rib over rice with pickled vegetables — a popular lunch box favourite

name: 雞腿便當 (jī tuǐ biàn dāng)
description: Braised or fried chicken leg bento with rice and sides — a classic Taiwanese railway meal

name: 油飯 (yóu fàn)
description: Glutinous rice stir-fried with pork, mushrooms, and dried shrimp — sticky, rich, and deeply savoury

name: 擔仔麵 (dàn zǎi miàn)
description: Tainan-style shrimp-broth noodle bowl with minced pork and a single shrimp — small but magnificent

name: 鍋貼 (guō tiē)
description: Pan-fried potsticker dumplings with a crispy skirt — satisfying in mild and cool-damp weather alike

name: 台南牛肉湯 (tái nán niú ròu tāng)
description: Tainan-style raw-sliced beef poured over with scalding broth — silky, mineral, extraordinary

name: 爌肉飯 (kuǎn ròu fàn)
description: Slow-braised pork knuckle slice over rice with pickled greens — rich, fatty, deeply comforting

name: 蚵仔麵線 (ô-á-mī-suànn)
description: Thick oyster vermicelli in starchy dark broth with intestines and coriander — quintessential street food

name: 鴨肉飯 (yā ròu fàn)
description: Sliced braised duck over rice with ginger broth on the side — lean and fragrant

name: 豆漿油條 (dòu jiāng yóu tiáo)
description: Warm soy milk with a crunchy fried breadstick — the definitive Taiwanese breakfast on a mild morning

name: 虱目魚粥 (shī mù yú zhōu)
description: Milkfish congee with ginger, sesame oil, and spring onion — silky and restorative

name: 牛肉麵 (niú ròu miàn)
description: Slow-braised beef shank in rich soy or clear broth with thick wheat noodles — Taiwan's national dish

name: 麻油雞 (má yóu jī)
description: Chicken braised in toasted sesame oil and rice wine with old ginger — warming and aromatic

name: 藥燉排骨 (yào dùn pái gǔ)
description: Pork ribs slow-simmered with Chinese herbal medicine — deeply nourishing on damp, cold days

name: 火鍋 (huǒ guō)
description: Communal hot pot with clear or spicy broth — ultimate comfort for grey, rainy days

name: 鹹粥 (xián zhōu)
description: Savoury rice porridge with minced pork, dried shrimp, and peanuts — light yet warming on rainy mornings

name: 四神湯 (sì shén tāng)
description: Pork intestine soup with four Chinese herbs (lotus seeds, yam, poria, euryale) — a gentle digestive tonic

name: 肉骨茶 (ròu gǔ chá)
description: Bak kut teh — pork ribs simmered in aromatic herbal broth with garlic and white pepper

name: 花生湯 (huā shēng tāng)
description: Warm peanut sweet soup with tender whole peanuts — a comforting Taiwanese classic on overcast afternoons

name: 米粉湯 (mǐ fěn tāng)
description: Fine rice noodles in pork bone broth with cabbage and fried shallots — a simple, satisfying bowl

name: 台式泡菜鍋 (tái shì pào cài guō)
description: Taiwanese kimchi-style hot pot with pork belly and tofu — tangy, spicy, and deeply warming

name: 烏龍麵 (wū lóng miàn)
description: Thick udon noodles in pork dashi broth with fish cake and spring onion — a hearty rainy-day bowl

name: 當歸鴨 (dāng guī yā)
description: Duck braised with angelica root and Chinese herbs — rich, earthy, and deeply warming

name: 紅豆湯 (hóng dòu tāng)
description: Warm red bean sweet soup with glutinous rice balls — comforting on a damp, grey afternoon

name: 芋頭排骨湯 (yù tóu pái gǔ tāng)
description: Taro and pork rib soup with ginger — creamy taro melts into the broth, deeply soothing

name: 蚵仔湯 (ô-á-tāng)
description: Oyster broth with ginger and basil — mineral-sweet and warming without being heavy

name: 薑汁地瓜湯 (jiāng zhī dì guā tāng)
description: Sweet potato soup spiced with fresh ginger juice — warming, sweet, and energising on damp days

name: 客家粄條 (kè jiā bǎn tiáo)
description: Hakka flat rice noodles in clear pork broth with dried shrimp and preserved turnip

name: 台灣滷味麵 (tái wān lǔ wèi miàn)
description: Noodles with a variety of braised toppings — eggs, tofu, bamboo — in a rich master stock

name: 酸辣湯 (suān là tāng)
description: Hot and sour soup with tofu, wood-ear mushroom, and egg — bracing and clearing on wet days

name: 豬肝湯 (zhū gān tāng)
description: Thinly sliced pork liver in ginger-sesame broth — tender, iron-rich, and restorative

name: 紅燒豆腐 (hóng shāo dòu fǔ)
description: Braised tofu in savoury soy and chilli sauce — satisfying protein on overcast days

name: 麵線糊 (mī-suànn-kô)
description: Thick vermicelli stew with oysters, pork intestine, and coriander — comforting and deeply savoury

name: 台式薑燒豬肉飯 (tái shì jiāng shāo zhū ròu fàn)
description: Ginger-glazed pork slices over rice with shredded cabbage — Japanese-influenced, popular in tea shops

name: 鮮魚湯 (xiān yú tāng)
description: Fresh seasonal fish simmered in clear ginger broth — clean, delicate, and nourishing

name: 薑母鴨 (jiāng mǔ yā)
description: Duck stewed in aged ginger and sesame oil — a warming ritual reserved for the coldest winter nights

name: 羊肉爐 (yáng ròu lú)
description: Lamb hot pot with Chinese herbs and bean curd — rich and deeply warming, perfect below 12°C

name: 麻辣鍋 (má là guō)
description: Sichuan numbing-spicy hot pot with beef, tripe, and vegetables — fiery and deeply warming

name: 燒酒雞 (shāo jiǔ jī)
description: Whole chicken simmered in rice wine with Chinese herbs — deeply warming and nourishing in cold weather

name: 花生豬腳湯 (huā shēng zhū jiǎo tāng)
description: Peanut and pig trotter soup slow-cooked until gelatinous — rich collagen broth, ideal for cold days

name: 薑汁豬腳 (jiāng zhī zhū jiǎo)
description: Braised pig's trotter with ginger and vinegar — sticky, warming, and traditionally eaten in winter

name: 紅燒獅子頭 (hóng shāo shī zi tóu)
description: Large pork meatballs braised in soy sauce with cabbage — tender, rich, and deeply warming

name: 東坡肉 (dōng pō ròu)
description: Hangzhou-style slow-braised pork belly in soy, wine, and sugar — lacquered, meltingly tender

name: 高麗菜豬肉鍋 (gāo lì cài zhū ròu guō)
description: Napa cabbage and pork belly stew — simple, clean, and warming; a family winter staple

name: 砂鍋魚頭 (shā guō yú tóu)
description: Fish head in clay pot with tofu, napa cabbage, and chilli — rich fish stock, warming and hearty

name: 烤地瓜 (kǎo dì guā)
description: Whole baked sweet potato from a roadside cart — caramelised skin, steaming interior, perfect winter snack

name: 薑茶湯圓 (jiāng chá tāng yuán)
description: Glutinous rice balls in spiced ginger broth — sweet, chewy, and intensely warming on frigid days

name: 花雕雞 (huā diāo jī)
description: Whole chicken braised in Shaoxing Huadiao wine — fragrant, amber-coloured, and warming

name: 牛腩煲 (niú nǎn bāo)
description: Braised beef brisket and tendon stew in clay pot — rich, gelatinous, satisfying on cold nights

name: 紅燒豬腳 (hóng shāo zhū jiǎo)
description: Red-braised pig trotter in soy, five spice, and rock sugar — sticky, collagen-rich winter comfort food

name: 台式雞湯 (tái shì jī tāng)
description: Taiwanese ginger-scallion chicken broth with rice wine — clear, golden, deeply restorative

name: 沙茶牛肉鍋 (shā chá niú ròu guō)
description: Shacha (satay) beef hot pot with Chinese spinach — rich, smoky sauce, deeply satisfying

name: 芋泥鴨 (yù ní yā)
description: Braised duck with creamy taro mash — earthy, rich, and perfect for a cold winter dinner

name: 豆腐煮魚 (dòu fǔ zhǔ yú)
description: Silken tofu simmered with fresh fish and ginger — light yet warming, easy on the stomach

name: 麻油腰花 (má yóu yāo huā)
description: Pork kidney stir-fried in sesame oil and old ginger — intensely warming and thought to nourish the kidneys

name: 三鮮湯 (sān xiān tāng)
description: Three-fresh soup with shrimp, squid, and fish cake in clear broth — light, briny, perfect in heat

name: 蔥爆牛肉 (cōng bào niú ròu)
description: Scallion-bursted beef stir-fry with soy and oyster sauce — fragrant, quick, and robust

name: 瓜仔肉飯 (guā zǐ ròu fàn)
description: Braised pork with pickled cucumber over rice — light, tangy, and summer-friendly comfort food

name: 芹菜豬肉水餃 (qín cài zhū ròu shuǐ jiǎo)
description: Celery and pork boiled dumplings — fragrant, hearty, welcome across mild and drizzly days

name: 筒仔米糕 (tǒng zǎi mǐ gāo)
description: Sticky glutinous rice with braised pork steamed in a bamboo cylinder — dense, savoury, and old-school

name: 蒸蛋 (zhēng dàn)
description: Silky steamed egg custard with soy and sesame — gentle, light, and easy to eat in warm weather

name: 鳳梨苦瓜雞湯 (fèng lí kǔ guā jī tāng)
description: Pineapple and bitter melon chicken soup — tangy-sweet and cooling, a classic summer detox broth

name: 起司豬排飯 (qǐ sī zhū pái fàn)
description: Japanese-style cheese pork cutlet over rice — crispy, melty, satisfying on grey days

name: 薑母茶配點心 (jiāng mǔ chá pèi diǎn xīn)
description: Aged ginger mother tea paired with sweet snacks — deeply warming tonic for chilly or damp days

name: 茄子豬肉煲 (qié zi zhū ròu bāo)
description: Eggplant and pork in clay pot with soy and oyster sauce — silky, rich, and warming on overcast days
